title: MERICS Data Insight: Chinese export prices
gist: A MERICS data snapshot finds that China's exports of high-end goods to the EU are rising in volume but their prices are not keeping pace, implying Chinese firms are moving upmarket while undercutting on price.

themes.theme: China's high-end export push into the EU

insights.claim: China's exports of high-end goods to the European Union are increasing, but export prices are not keeping pace with the rise in export volumes.
insights.speaker: MERICS
insights.so_what: Signals Chinese firms moving up the value chain while undercutting on price, squeezing European high-end manufacturers' margins.
insights.quote: prices do not keep pace with the increase in exports
insights.locator: In this edition of MERICS Data Insights

insights.claim: The divergence between rising high-end export volumes and lagging prices points to Chinese competitiveness in higher-value segments, not just low-cost goods.
insights.speaker: MERICS
insights.so_what: Undermines the assumption that Chinese overcapacity pressure is confined to commodity products; the threat is climbing into premium EU market segments.
insights.locator: She finds that prices do not keep pace
